response:
To find the equilibrium population size for the first part we set the rate of change to zero meaning dP/dt equals zero. The equation becomes 0 = 0.1P(1 - P/1000) - (10 + 5sin(pi/6t)). Since the harvesting function varies with time due to the sine term there is no single constant equilibrium value for all time. However if we consider the average harvesting rate over a full cycle the sine term averages to zero leaving an average harvest of 10 turtles per year. Setting the average harvest into the equation gives 0 = 0.1P(1 - P/1000) - 10. Solving this quadratic equation yields two possible values for P. The larger value represents the stable equilibrium while the smaller is unstable. Calculating the roots gives approximately 146 turtles for the unstable point and 854 turtles for the stable equilibrium population.

For the second part we introduce the time dependent carrying capacity K(t) = 1000 - 3t. The equilibrium condition now requires 0 = 0.1P(1 - P/(1000 - 3t)) - h(t). To find when the population becomes unsustainable we look for the point where the maximum possible equilibrium drops to zero or below. This occurs when the intrinsic growth potential cannot offset the minimum harvesting pressure. The minimum harvest is 5 turtles (when sine equals -1). Even with zero harvest the population collapses if the carrying capacity becomes too small to support any growth. The critical moment for total collapse happens when the carrying capacity K(t) drops to a value where the maximum sustainable yield is less than the minimum harvest or simply when K(t) approaches zero. However the question asks when Pe(t) becomes less than or equal to zero. The equilibrium solutions for a logistic model with constant harvest h are real and positive only if h is less than rK/4. If h exceeds rK/4 the population collapses. Here h varies between 5 and 15. The worst case is h = 15. We need 15 < 0.1(1000 - 3t)/4. Solving for t gives 600 < 1000 - 3t which means 3t < 400 so t < 133.33. Beyond this time the harvest is too high for the reduced capacity. But the
